name: Zarzalejo, El Escorial, Mirasierra, Galapagar – circular route
name_original: Zarzalejo, El Escorial, Mirasierra, Galapagar – ruta circular
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Zarzalejo are set within the Sierra Oeste of Madrid, characterized by mountainous granite reliefs, extensive pine forests, and chestnut groves. The region features significant natural landmarks such as the Las Machotas granite formations and the Valmayor Reservoir. This diverse terrain provides varied elevation changes and scenic backdrops for road cyclists. Best road cycling routes around Zarzalejo The most popular road cycling route is Zarzalejo, El Escorial, Mirasierra, Galapagar – circular route, a 43.1 miles (69.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 16 minutes to complete. This moderate route connects several towns and offers views of the surrounding landscape. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Robledo de Chavela, Valdemorillo, Navalagamella – circular route, a moderate 36.0 miles (58.0 km) path. This route passes through varied landscapes, including natural areas and local villages. Local road cyclists also love the Peralejo Church – Views of Valmayor Reservoir loop from Zarzalejo, a 31.5 miles (50.6 km) trail leading through areas with views of the Valmayor Reservoir, often completed in about 2 hours 20 minutes. Road cycling around Zarzalejo is defined by granite mountainous terrain, pine forests, and the presence of the Valmayor Reservoir. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Zarzalejo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from more than 30 reviews. More than 2,600 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Zarzalejo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling in Zarzalejo?
A: Road cycling in Zarzalejo offers a diverse landscape characterized by mountainous granite reliefs, extensive pine forests, and picturesque chestnut groves. You'll encounter varied elevation changes, from challenging climbs around formations like Las Machotas to scenic descents, often with views of the Valmayor Reservoir. The area is known for its granitic terrain, which provides both stunning scenery and unique architectural elements in towns like Zarzalejo Pueblo.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Zarzalejo?
A: There are over 60 road cycling routes available around Zarzalejo on komoot, catering to various skill levels. This includes a mix of easy, moderate, and difficult trails, ensuring there's something for every road cyclist.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of road cycling routes in Zarzalejo?
A: Zarzalejo offers a good range of difficulty levels for road cyclists. You'll find 1 easy route, 29 moderate routes, and 31 difficult routes, allowing you to choose trails that match your experience and fitness level.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints or landmarks to see along the road cycling routes?
A: Absolutely! The region is rich in scenic viewpoints and historical landmarks. You can cycle to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/740607" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Seat of Philip II&lt;/a&gt;, which offers spectacular views of the Monastery of El Escorial and the Guadarrama Valley. Many routes also provide impressive vistas of the Valmayor Reservoir and the prominent granite formations of Las Machotas. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24897144" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peralejo Church – Views of Valmayor Reservoir loop from Zarzalejo&lt;/a&gt; offers beautiful views of the reservoir.

Q: Can I find any natural monuments or unique geological features while cycling?
A: Yes, Zarzalejo's granitic landscape is home to several natural monuments and unique features. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3005039" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hornillo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, the intriguing &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6556720" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bear Cave&lt;/a&gt;, or the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6277005" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Stone slab above the Valmayor reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The entire area is characterized by its granite formations, including the prominent Machota Alta and Machota Baja.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Zarzalejo?
A: The road cycling routes around Zarzalejo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars from more than 30 reviews. Over 2,600 road cyclists have explored the area using komoot, often praising the diverse terrain, scenic beauty, and the mix of challenging climbs and enjoyable descents.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling routes in Zarzalejo?
A: While many routes in Zarzalejo feature significant elevation changes, there is at least one easy road cycling route available. For families, it's recommended to check the difficulty grade and elevation profile of specific routes to ensure they are suitable for all participants. The region's network includes options that can be adapted for less experienced riders.

Q: When is the best time of year for road cycling in Zarzalejo?
A: Zarzalejo, located in the Sierra Oeste of Madrid, generally offers pleasant cycling conditions during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be hot, especially at midday, so early morning or late afternoon rides are preferable. Winter can bring colder temperatures, but many days are still suitable for cycling, particularly in the lower elevations.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes that connect multiple towns?
A: Yes, Zarzalejo is well-integrated into a network of circular routes that connect various towns. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977041139/zarzalejo-el-escorial-mirasierra-galapagar-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zarzalejo, El Escorial, Mirasierra, Galapagar – circular route&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 43.1-mile (69.3 km) trail. Another favorite is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977182165/robledo-de-chavela-valdemorillo-navalagamella-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Robledo de Chavela, Valdemorillo, Navalagamella – circular route&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 36.0-mile (58.0 km) path passing through varied landscapes and local villages.

Q: What is the longest road cycling route available from Zarzalejo?
A: One of the longer and more challenging road cycling routes in the vicinity is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6928874" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Arrebatacapas – Puerto de la Lancha loop from Robledo de Chavela&lt;/a&gt;. This difficult route spans approximately 91.5 miles (147.2 km) with significant elevation gain, offering a substantial challenge for experienced cyclists.

Q: Are there options for long-distance road cycling in the Zarzalejo area?
A: Yes, the Zarzalejo area is ideal for long-distance road cycling, being part of larger networks like CiclaMadrid and the Imperial Route. Routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31521543" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz Verde Pass – La Escondida loop from Robledo de Chavela&lt;/a&gt;, at 68.8 miles (110.7 km), provide excellent opportunities for extended rides with considerable elevation gain, connecting various mountain passes and scenic areas.
tips: The honey from this town is a product of considerable quality. You may also be interested in seeing the church of Santiago Ápostol, the fountain and piles of Navazo and the fountain of Pozuelo, the hermitage of the Virgen de la Soledad, the traditional houses of the Santiago neighborhood and the old copper and malachite mines.
Charming place to enjoy the tranquility of the reservoir. At certain times of the year there are concentrations of aquatic birds that rest on their migratory journeys.

Peralejo is a town of restaurants and cafeterias where you can rest for a while and recharge your batteries. It also has the Cañada Real Nature Center, where wild animals with injuries are treated. In the surrounding area there are birds of prey, day and night, eagles, vultures, wolves, etc.
